goal: Review the `elara` repository at /Users/michaeltimpe/Downloads/local-llm/luxebox/elara. Start all file reads relative to this path. Start by listing the root with `list_dir` and reading any README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/SECURITY/docs files. Then systematically look for: (1) security issues — input handling, auth, secrets, injection, deserialization, path traversal, dependency vulns; (2) correctness bugs — error handling, race conditions, resource leaks, silent failures; (3) robustness — missing timeouts/retries, unbounded loops; (4) maintainability issues that mask real risk. End with a severity-grouped markdown report. Ground every finding in code you read via tools — no invented filenames or quotes.

subtasks.title: List the root directory of the repository
Read any README, ARCHITECTURE, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, and docs files
Search for security issues — input handling, auth, secrets, injection, deserialization, path traversal, dependency vulns
Search for correctness bugs — error handling, race conditions, resource leaks, silent failures
Check for robustness issues — missing timeouts/retries, unbounded loops
Identify maintainability issues that mask real risk
Generate a severity-grouped markdown report based on findings
